name: docent-agent
description: docent — narrated, animated explainer films for any subject (a codebase, a PR, an essay, a wiki, a URL). Four mode-centric slash commands: /docent-doctor (first-run bootstrap and health check), /docent-pr (PR-review film), /docent-ar (architecture-review film), /docent-explain (the one-shot — any subject, any mode, end to end). Use when the user asks to review a codebase as a film, explain a subject as a video, review a pull request at depth, or hands over a subject expecting an artifact at the end.

interface.shortDescription: Turn any subject into a narrated, animated explainer film
interface.longDescription:
Point docent at a codebase, a pull request, an essay, a wiki, or a URL — any subject — and it produces a narrated, animated film that interrogates the subject. Not a tour that admires it. Every film is graded by an adversarial sub-agent before it ships.

Four skills:
  /docent-doctor   — first-run bootstrap and ongoing health check
  /docent-pr       — PR-review film (load-bearing 5%, the trade-off, a verdict)
  /docent-ar       — architecture-review film (components, flow, failure modes)
  /docent-explain  — the one-shot, any subject, any mode

First /docent-doctor invocation clones the engine to ~/.local/share/docent/engine, installs every cascade dependency (uv, ffmpeg, Kokoro voice weights, Remotion), and puts a `docent` CLI on PATH.
defaultPrompt: Review the architecture of https://github.com/owner/repo as a docent film
Set up docent on this machine
